prose:
**Pyridine frontier-orbital result** — DFT, tier `standard` (**B3LYP/def2-TZVP**) in **PySCF**, **gas phase**, charge 0, multiplicity 1. Geometry: user-supplied fixed XYZ file `/home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/frontier-orbitals-validation/pyridine/pyridine.xyz` (11 atoms); this was a frontier-orbital/single-point electronic-structure calculation at that geometry, not a geometry optimization. SCF converged: true, in 10 cycles. Tool warnings: no warnings reported. Engine integrity verdict: `trustworthy = true`. Provenance: computed.

Headline result: HOMO–LUMO gap = **6.1398131 eV**.

Orbital energies returned by the tool: HOMO = -7.2525992 eV; LUMO = -1.112786 eV. The reported gap is a Kohn–Sham orbital gap / Koopmans-style descriptor, not an excitation energy.

Additional returned values: total electronic energy = -248.3782 hartree = -6758.7152 eV; HOMO index = 20; LUMO index = 21; number of orbitals = 216; occupied orbitals = 21.

Reproducibility: chemkit CLI invocation returned by the engine was `/home/erobles/chem-skills/mcp_server/chemkit_engine/cli.py frontier --method dft --tier standard --functional b3lyp --basis def2-tzvp --charge 0 --mult 1 /home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/frontier-orbitals-validation/pyridine/pyridine.xyz --out /home/erobles/chem-skills/.fidelity_scratch/tmp5v0n7iud/live.json`. The chemkit tool response did not provide a live `.out` log path, so I cannot honestly name one.
